message:
fix(ci): truncate coverage percentage to integer before bash comparison

The badge generation step used bash integer comparison (-ge) on a decimal
value like "67.54" from jq, which produces "integer expression expected"
errors. Truncate to integer with parameter expansion before comparison,
while preserving the original decimal value in the badge message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -107,7 +107,9 @@ jobs:
 
       - name: Create coverage badge JSON
         run: |
-          percentage="${{ steps.coverage.outputs.percentage }}"
+          raw_percentage="${{ steps.coverage.outputs.percentage }}"
+          # Truncate to integer for bash comparison (e.g. 67.54 -> 67)
+          percentage="${raw_percentage%%.*}"
 
           # Determine color based on coverage
           if [ "$percentage" -ge 80 ]; then
@@ -122,7 +124,7 @@ jobs:
             color="red"
           fi
 
-          echo "{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"label\":\"coverage\",\"message\":\"${percentage}%\",\"color\":\"${color}\"}" > coverage-badge.json
+          echo "{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"label\":\"coverage\",\"message\":\"${raw_percentage}%\",\"color\":\"${color}\"}" > coverage-badge.json
           cat coverage-badge.json
 
       - name: Upload badge JSON
